feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: One Type of Exercise Can Give You Better Results With Less Effort, Expert Says
entry.summary: Exercising smarter rather than harder could lead to more effective muscle training, offering those of us short on time or energy an alternative way to maximize gains from workouts.

entry.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
entry.summary: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

entry.title: A New Atlas Reveals Hidden Details of The Human Body Like Never Before
entry.summary: A pioneering project has revealed the human body like never before, from entire organs down to cellular structures, with unprecedented precision on the scale of a single micron – about 50 times thinner than a strand of human hair.

entry.title: 1 in 4 people are ‘Alzheimer’s gene’ carriers like Chris Hemsworth. What to do
entry.summary: APOE4 comes with a higher risk for Alzheimer’s disease, but the 25 per cent of people who have it can lower that risk with lifestyle changes.

entry.title: Millions are losing weight on Ozempic and Wegovy — here's what an orthopedic surgeon wants every patient to know
entry.summary: Losing weight on Ozempic? An orthopedic surgeon explains what GLP-1 drugs really do to your bones, joints, and muscles and how to stay safe.

entry.title: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
entry.summary: The World Health Organization said one case of hantavirus infection has been confirmed, with five more suspected cases under investigation.

entry.title: Immune cells leave ‘memory’ of obesity after weight loss
entry.summary: The author of the study said the body's "memory" could still leave people who have lost weight at risk of health issues associated with obesity.

entry.title: Researchers Unveil Groundbreaking Sustainable Solution to Vitamin B12 Deficiency
entry.summary: A modified form of Spirulina may offer a more sustainable source of vitamin B12.

entry.title: How to spot and prevent the deadliest form of skin cancer
entry.summary: If caught early, it has a 99% cure rate.

entry.title: Family data reveal two genetic paths to childhood depression and anxiety
entry.summary: Many common mental health disorders, including depression and anxiety, are associated with a tendency to internalize problems or, in other words, to direct feelings inwards instead of expressing them and sharing them with others. Past studies suggest that thi…

entry.title: Scientists just discovered what coffee is really doing to your gut and brain
entry.summary: Coffee doesn’t just energize—it actively reshapes the gut and mind. Researchers found that both caffeinated and decaf coffee altered gut bacteria in ways linked to better mood and lower stress. Decaf even improved learning and memory, while caffeine boosted f…

entry.title: My Boyfriend Turned My Biggest Fantasy Into My Worst Nightmare. I’m This Close to Ending It.
entry.summary: It was supposed to be the group sex of our dreams.

entry.title: Heart doctor shares warning over food ‘worse than smoking’ that decreases lifespan
entry.summary: Eating high amounts is linked to a greater risk of high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, heart attacks and strokes, and early death

entry.title: 4 Daily Exercises That Firm Belly Overhang Faster Than Gym Workouts After 60
entry.summary: An athletic trainer shares 4 daily moves that strengthen your deep core and reduce belly overhang, especially for adults over 60.

entry.title: Mass General Brigham, Dana-Farber dig into early cancer detection efforts — but how much is too much?
entry.summary: Doctors at the centers are coordinating not only which tests patients would benefit from, but what, if anything, they should do with the results.

entry.title: A tool to help keep dementia in check
entry.summary: Many people fear that a family history of dementia dooms them to inevitably suffer the condition themselves. But a new tool, the Brain Care Score, shows how lifestyle changes can be beneficial, cutting the risk of dementia.

entry.title: Researchers Discover Boosting a Single Protein Helps the Brain Fight Alzheimer’s
entry.summary: New research suggests the brain may already possess underutilized mechanisms to combat Alzheimer’s disease.

entry.title: Subtle signs you might be vitamin D deficient, and what your body is trying to tell you
entry.summary: Vitamin D deficiency rarely announces itself with drama. It builds slowly, almost quietly, until small changes begin to feel like a pattern. A lingering tiredness. A low mood that does not quite lift. A body that feels heavier than usual. These are easy to di…

entry.title: Eating Eggs May Help Boost Your Memory, According to a Recent Study
entry.summary: If eggs are your favorite breakfast staple, you're in luck. A new study found that eating eggs may aid in the fight against cognitive decline as we age, particularly for women. Here's what you need to know.

entry.title: California researchers devise treatment for deadly pregnancy condition
entry.summary: A new study finds a promising treatment approach may help women with a dangerous pregnancy condition called preeclampsia.

entry.title: AI Detects “Invisible” Signs of Pancreatic Cancer Years Before Diagnosis
entry.summary: An AI system may spot pancreatic cancer long before it becomes visible.

entry.title: This New Material Kills Viruses the Moment They Touch It
entry.summary: Scientists have engineered a flexible plastic film that destroys viruses on contact, without a single drop of disinfectant.

entry.title: Sudden back pain? It might not be muscle strain; here’s how to spot kidney stones early and what to do
entry.summary: A sudden stab in the lower back is easy to dismiss. Long hours at a desk, a bad mattress, or an awkward twist often take the blame. But not every back pain begins in the muscles. Sometimes, the body is flagging something deeper and more urgent: the kidneys. K…

entry.title: Bay Area’s mosquito season is starting early. An invasive species could make it a lot worse
entry.summary: Mild weather is fueling an earlier mosquito surge — and officials are racing to stop an invasive species from exploding in parts of the East Bay.

entry.title: This 1 Hobby Is Great For An Aging Brain, According To A Study
entry.summary: And no one is too old to pick it up.

entry.title: The Ripeness Level Of Your Banana Actually Matters For Your Health — Here's How
entry.summary: The nutritional content in a banana changes as it ripens. What ripeness level is best for you?

entry.title: Six of the best exercises to preserve strength for GLP-1 users, according to an expert
entry.summary: Protect muscle and bone health with these moves

entry.title: 10 Flavonoid-Rich Foods to Fight Inflammation
entry.summary: Found in a variety of fruits and vegetables, flavonoids are powerful antioxidants with anti-inflammatory properties.

entry.title: 4 subtle signs you aren’t eating enough protein
entry.summary: The macronutrient is important for maintaining muscle as you age, but it’s possible you’re not getting as much as you think.

entry.title: The body’s most mysterious organ may play a key role in longevity and cancer
entry.summary: A raft of research is recasting the thymus from a bit player to a potent regulator of aging and immune health.

entry.title: Tamannaah Bhatia's fitness trainer shares healthy recipe for high-protein chocolate peanut butter bowl | Hindustan Times
entry.summary: Fitness trainer shares easy no-cooking breakfast bowl recipe that has zero added sugar and is loaded with 45 grams of protein.

entry.title: Brain health: Even a little alcohol impairs brain blood flow
entry.summary: Even people who never exceed the recommended limits for alcohol consumption are likely to experience brain health problems as they age, a new study suggests.

entry.title: It's probably already in your fridge — and it could help in cutting risk of Alzheimer's
entry.summary: Eating seafood twice a week can be important to protect the brain from different diseases.

entry.title: ‘A tough one.’ Boston’s allergy season came late but is hitting hard.
entry.summary: Doctors said itchy, watery eyes, symptoms of allergic conjunctivitis, are especially bad this year.

entry.title: “Not just living longer, but living better”—a certified trainer shares five things that matter most to aging well and longevity
entry.summary: Address these five factors now to stay younger as you age

entry.title: “You Are Just Fat”: 65 Patients Who Couldn’t Believe The Treatment They Got In The ER
entry.summary: If you're heading to the emergency room, there's a good chance you're not doing it just for kicks. It's not exactly a fun place to hang out on a Friday night and make new friends.

entry.title: Two Supplements For Menopause May Actually Help, Expert Reveals
entry.summary: Social media is saturated with menopause solutions: powders for brain fog, gummies for sleep, or capsules promising hormonal balance.

entry.title: Brain scans of 800 incarcerated men link psychopathy to an expanded cortical surface area
entry.summary: A large-scale brain imaging study of over 800 incarcerated men reveals that highly psychopathic individuals possess an expanded cortical surface area and a compressed physical brain organization, offering new clues into the biology of empathy and antisocial b…

entry.title: Scientists Uncover Fatal Weakness in “Zombie Cells” Linked to Cancer
entry.summary: A newly identified weakness in “zombie” cells may open the door to more precise cancer treatments by turning their own survival strategy against them.

entry.title: Dog Attack on State Line Road Leaves Woman Seriously Injured; Two Dogs Still on the Loose
entry.summary: Dog Attack on State Line Road Leaves Woman Seriously Injured; Two Dogs Still on the Loose SEE URGENT PUBLIC SAFETY ALERT BELOW DOG DESCRIPTIONS: Mostly black with some white around the chest area. Lab looking mixes. UPDATE: The primary aggressive dog (black …

entry.title: World-First Study Reveals Human Hearts Can Regenerate After a Heart Attack
entry.summary: For the first time, scientists have shown that heart muscle cells can regrow after a heart attack in humans, a process that had previously only been observed in mice.

entry.title: The Roots of Dementia Trace Back All The Way to Childhood, Experts Reveal
entry.summary: Dementia is often associated with older people, but it doesn't just appear out of nowhere.

entry.title: How to sleep better, from bedtimes to regularity – what the research says
entry.summary: Sleep research has come a long way in the last decade. Here’s what you need to know to help make your slumber better.

entry.title: 'I’m 99 and train six days a week – here’s everything I do to stay strong and mobile'
entry.summary: Shirley Goldman says staying active keeps her strong and independent with age – here's exactly how she trains and eats, plus her training tips.

entry.title: This Simple Home Device May Boost Brain Power in Adults Over 40
entry.summary: HEPA air purifiers can slightly boost brain function in adults 40+, potentially reducing pollution-related cognitive decline. Using a HEPA air purifier for one month may modestly improve cognitive performance in adults over 40, particularly in areas like ment…

entry.title: Scientists Develop New Antibody For Virus That Infects 95% of People
entry.summary: The Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) is one of the world's most common viruses, thought to be lurking in 95 percent of adults.

entry.title: Woman, 21, Was Dismissed by Doctors for Years and Told to Lose Weight. More Than a Decade Later, She Learned What Was Wrong
entry.summary: The woman claims doctors' comments about her weight led her to spiral into "disordered eating"

entry.title: Omega-3 Supplements May Increase Risk of Cognitive Decline, Scientists Warn
entry.summary: Nutritional supplements are exploding in popularity, but some experts remain skeptical of the numerous health claims surrounding these pills and their safety at high dosages.

entry.title: What Eating Yogurt Every Day Does to Your Body, According to Nutrition Experts
entry.summary: Eating yogurt every day will benefit your bones, gut, and muscle growth, say nutrition experts. It will also support muscle repair and satiety, helping you stay full for longer. For the most health benefits, the recommend plain and unflavored yogurt.

entry.title: Diagnosed with colon cancer at 36, she spent her last years helping others with the disease
entry.summary: The daughter of Iranian immigrants, Asal Sayas worked in the White House and Senate, lobbied for AIDS research and became a tireless champion for people with cancer.
